issues.description: Round-1 verifier claimed the switch-to-full router-config guardrail checks ai_router/router-config.yaml relative to set.root, which it suspected is the session-set folder, so installed routers at <workspace>/ai_router/router-config.yaml would still trigger the missing-router warning.
issues.verificationMethod: Round-1 static inspection of the diff (which did not include fileSystem.ts, where SessionSet.root is assigned).
issues.suggestedTestOrCheck: Trace SessionSet.root from readSessionSets() in src/utils/fileSystem.ts.
issues.resolution_notes: False positive - root-cause was a context gap (fileSystem.ts was not in the diff). readSessionSets(root) scans path.join(root, 'docs/session-sets') (fileSystem.ts:420) and assigns that same root to SessionSet.root (fileSystem.ts:761), so set.root IS the workspace root, exactly the base D4 wants; openFile.ts's findPlaywrightTests uses the identical convention. Round-2 gpt-5-4 re-verify accepted the disproof: VERIFIED.

issues.description: Round-1 verifier flagged malformed tier scalar handling: (a) switchTier's early return on target === set.config.tier means a typo'd scalar (tier: ful, parsed as full) is never repaired on a same-tier pick; (b) it also claimed case-variant values like tier: LIGHTWEIGHT are treated as already-target while parseSessionSetConfig would read them as full.
issues.verificationMethod: Round-1 static inspection of switchTier.ts and tierRewrite.ts against the described parser behavior.
issues.suggestedTestOrCheck: Compare with parseSessionSetConfig's tier branch (fileSystem.ts:251-257); unit-test the same-tier typo-repair path.
issues.resolution_notes: Half (a) accepted and fixed: the command's early same-tier return was removed - rewriteSpecTier is now the single decision authority, so a same-tier pick over a malformed scalar rewrites it to the canonical value (distinct 'Repaired' message; new matrix test covers it). Half (b) disproven: parseSessionSetConfig lowercases before validating (fileSystem.ts:253 'const v = tier.toLowerCase()'), so tier: LIGHTWEIGHT parses as lightweight and the helper's lowercased comparison matches the parser exactly (new matrix test pins this). TS suite 752 pass + 2 pre-existing Set-026 fails after the fix. Round-2 gpt-5-4 re-verify accepted both halves: VERIFIED.
